name: French Breakfast Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg.
2. In a food processor, insert the steel blade. Add butter and sugar, using the on/off method to cream them together. Add egg and mix well.
3. Alternately feed through the tube the dry ingredients and milk, ensuring that milk is the last ingredient added. Fill greased muffin tins about 2/3 full.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from the muffin tin and roll in melted butter, then in a mixture of 1/2 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon.
5. Do not roll the puffs if you do not plan to use them immediately. They can be frozen or refrigerated and reheated by rolling in the butter and sugar mixture again. The puffs will become hard and crusty if rolled in butter and then reheated. Serve hot.
Note: These French Breakfast Puffs are traditionally served with homemade butter and coffee.
